formality.description: Czech has a T-V distinction: vy (vykání, formal) vs ty (tykání, informal). Professional software uses vy-form.

gender.inclusiveGuidance: Czech has grammatical gender affecting nouns, adjectives, and past-tense verbs. When gender is unknown, masculine is the conventional default. Slash notation (e.g., 'přihlášen/a') can be used where space permits.

formal-vy.label: Vykání (Formal)
formal-vy.description: Standard for professional software and business communication.
formal-vy.prompt: Formal Czech. Use vykání (vy-form) consistently. Professional register for software interfaces.

informal-ty.label: Tykání (Informal)
informal-ty.description: For consumer apps, social media, and casual contexts.
informal-ty.prompt: Informal Czech. Use tykání (ty-form). Conversational tone for consumer products.
